title: Command Pattern Undo Stack
synthesis_notes: The command-history approach is strongest due to existing repo patterns.

directions.name: Command History
directions.rationale: Reuses existing command pattern infrastructure with minimal surface area.
directions.raw_phase3_response: Implement a command stack that records each action as an invertible command object.
directions.approach_summary: Wrap each editor action in a command object with do/undo methods; maintain a bounded history stack.
known_risks: Memory pressure from large history stacks

directions.name: Event Sourcing
directions.rationale: Provides full audit log but introduces significant complexity versus command pattern.
directions.raw_phase3_response: Store all mutations as immutable events; replay events to reconstruct state.
directions.approach_summary: Replace mutable state with an append-only event log; replay to any point.
